paper.title: Hypervelocity Stars Trace a Supermassive Black Hole in the Large Magellanic Cloud

extraction.summary: Extracted 21 unbound B-type main-sequence stars from the HVS Survey (Brown et al. 2014), reassessed by Han et al. (2025) with Gaia DR3 proper motions for Galactic Center vs LMC origin

method_chain.summary: HVS Survey photometric and spectroscopic catalog of blue halo sources (Brown et al. 2014), supplemented with Gaia DR3 astrometry
outputs: HVS Survey stars with 6D phase-space measurements

source_refs.context: HVS Survey data and Gaia DR3 proper motions

method_chain.summary: Cross-match HVS Survey stars with Gaia DR3
outputs: Gaia DR3 source_ids and proper motions

source_refs.context: supplement with Gaia DR3 proper motions

method_chain.summary: Quality cuts based on RUWE values close to 1

source_refs.context: Table caption mentioning RUWE values close to 1

method_chain.summary: Heliocentric distances from stellar model fitting to observed temperatures and surface gravities (Brown et al. 2014)

source_refs.context: Distances inferred from fitting temperatures and surface gravities with stellar models

method_chain.summary: Spectroscopic radial velocities from the HVS Survey follow-up

source_refs.context: HVS Survey spectroscopic follow-up

method_chain.summary: Compute Galactocentric velocities from 6D phase-space data

source_refs.context: Orbit integration back in time from 6D phase-space location

method_chain.summary: Adopt GC19 Simulation 7 for the MW-LMC system with time-dependent gravitational potential

source_refs.context: Fiducial model for MW-LMC system from GC19 Simulation 7

method_chain.summary: Backward orbit integration for 400 Myr in the time-varying MW-LMC potential

source_refs.context: Integrate orbits back in time for 400 Myr

method_chain.summary: Original HVS Survey unbound classification based on Galactocentric radial velocity exceeding local escape velocity

source_refs.context: HVS Survey discovered 21 unbound B-type stars

method_chain.summary: Hypothesis test for Galactic Center vs LMC origin using Mahalanobis distance and likelihood ratio

source_refs.context: Hypothesis test for GC vs LMC origin using Mahalanobis distance

method_chain.summary: Classify HVSs by likely origin: GC-only, LMC-only, both GC-favored, both LMC-favored, or ambiguous

source_refs.context: Classification of HVSs by origin in pie chart and table

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS 1 is one of the 21 unbound B-type main-sequence stars from the HVS Survey (Brown et al. 2014), reassessed in this paper with Gaia DR3 proper motions for its origin
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly describes all 21 HVS Survey stars as unbound hypervelocity stars and provides origin reassessment for each

source_refs.context: Abstract: HVS Survey detected 21 unbound B-type main-sequence stars in the Milky Way outer halo
Methods: HVS Survey data includes 21 unbound late B-type main sequence stars with Gaia DR3 proper motions
Conclusion: investigated origin of 21 unbound B-type main sequence stars
Introduction: discovery of 21 main-sequence B stars suspected to be unbound by Brown et al. 2014
Methods: analyze complete HVS Survey data from Brown 2014

citation_context_refs.context: Citation to Brown 2014 for HVS Survey unbound B stars discovery

hypothesis_metrics.description: p-value for the hypothesis that the HVS originates from the Galactic Center
p-value for the hypothesis that the HVS originates from the LMC center
Log-likelihood ratio log(L_LMC / L_MW); positive values favor LMC origin, negative favor MW origin
hypothesis_metrics.hypothesis: LMC vs MW origin likelihood ratio
